title: CSS Nesting
description: Native CSS nesting lets you write child selectors, pseudo-classes, and media queries inside a parent rule using &. The & represents the parent selector — no preprocessor needed. Baseline newly available since 2023.
html:
Card One
Hover to see the border change.
Card Two
The hover state is nested inside .card.
Card Three
No separate selector block needed.
challenge: The cards have a hover transition set up but no hover state defined yet. Inside the .card rule, write &:hover { border-color: #a855f7; } to highlight the border on hover — without leaving the .card block.

alternatives.name: Nesting media queries
alternatives.description: You can also nest @media queries inside a rule — a major Sass pattern that is now native CSS. The styles apply to .card only when both conditions are met.

realWorldUsage: Component-scoped CSS — keeping a card's base styles, hover state, child element styles, and breakpoint overrides all in one block. Previously required Sass or a CSS-in-JS library. Now standard in vanilla CSS files and CSS Modules.
